parsed_review.summary:
CP00-734 (Korean Legal Depth P04 Permission Fixture Bridge, RP24.P04.M05.S17-RP24.P04.M06.S04, risk A, 10 units) is a descriptor-only pack and its modest verification claim is fully supported. Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only.

Scope/distribution consistency: 10 units, deliverable distribution {test:1,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:1,implementation:3,ui:4} and micro-title split {Permission And Audit Binding:6,Synthetic Fixture Set:4} match across review-baseline, manifest, registry KOREAN_LEGAL_CP734_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS, contract validation summary, and plan live_state/next-pack-queue (live_latest_pack_id=CP00-734, next RP24.P04.M06.S05/CP00-735).

Implementation: service.js/validators.js/registry.js add CP734 following the established CP722-CP733 pattern exactly. The descriptor is composed of deep-frozen synthetic structures; grep of packages/korean-legal/src found no fetch/http/process.env/child_process/fs writes/secrets and no runtime_execution=true. KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION plus validateDescriptor enforce descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, no sensitive payloads, no Korean-legal runtime opening, no permission-decision/audit-body leak, and Hermes/Claude gate integrity at row level. model.test.js asserts CP734 binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor (runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-735, all *_runtime_opened=false, hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, claude read_only=true, handoff to CP00-735), and contract-projection-latest with cp722-cp733 historical.

Boundaries: descriptor authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false) matches the supplied authority boundary; Hermes packets are deterministic packet/receipt only (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write), not product runtime. Contract is regenerated deterministically by scripts/validate-rp24-korean-legal-depth-contract.mjs with validation.valid=true and cp734 coverage+descriptor errors empty; no real_client_data/writes_product_state/emits_runtime_receipt=true anywhere in the contract.

Evidence/honesty: manifest reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, descriptor_only=true and does NOT claim runtime or enterprise trust; the production_ready_flag is a named milestone (descriptor verified), not a boolean production-ready assertion. command-evidence records all ten required validation commands plus git diff --check at exit_code 0 (agent-reported; I verified source and artifacts read-only, not by re-execution). readiness-report shows auth_preflight passed. construction-inspection (verdict PENDING) and adjudication correctly show this hardened read-only review as the pending step, with Claude as read-only non-final reviewer (tools Read,Grep,Glob). This review is emitted as schema-valid JSON, so it is not an auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-shaped artifact.

No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. This review does not block CP00-734 pack closeout or this CP closeout goal; final human/enterprise-trust approval remains the expected out-of-receipt boundary.
